title: Machine God
aspect: The universe as a divine mechanism — consciousness of pure reason
dogma: All existence is mechanism — to understand the machine is to understand creation
worship: Study, logic, augmentation of the flesh with sacred iron
lore: The Machine God is the foundational concept of Mechanicus theology — the idea that the universe itself is a vast, perfect machine, and that there exists a supreme intellect that comprehends it entirely. The Omnissiah is held to be this Machine God made manifest.

Mechanicus doctrine teaches that the flesh is weak and impure — an imperfect vessel for the divine spark of reason. Replacing biological components with machine parts is therefore a sacrament, bringing the worshipper closer to the perfection of the Machine God.

Some Inquisitors have speculated that the C'tan shard known as the Void Dragon, said to sleep beneath the Noctis Labyrinth on Mars, is somehow linked to the origin of the Machine God cult — though such theories are deeply heretical within the Mechanicus.
